ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-29-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: John McGrath appeared in person and Ken Horowitz via zoom for and with Carmel, Rachel, and Aiden Kavanaugh in person (petitioner/guardian of estate).
Maureen McFadden for and with Timothy Breen (GAL) appeared via zoom.

Discussion was held regarding the memorandum of agreement to leave family law conflict seperate, that the sale of the house to be treated as separate property or community property, that the $1,100,000.00 proceeds of the sale of property located on San Jacinto would be split in the amount of $337,000.00 for each person, and that bond needs to be addressed. Amended petition for approval of first and final account etc., is taken under submission.

Judge: Ross Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Maloney. Not reported.

Petition Of Administrator Carmel Kavanagh To Approve Creditors Claim Of Administrator
Off Calendar May-02-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John McGrath, in person, for and with, Carmel Kavanagh, Administrator; Maureen McFadden, via Zoom for and with Tim Breen, Guardian Ad Litem ("GAL"); Nancy Mass, via Zoom, Ms. Kavanagh's Family Law Attorney.

The court acknowledges reading Mr. McGrath's brief and advises the parties of its tentative ruling and whether either counsel has anything to add. Mr. McGrath does not, and Ms. McFadden submits on the papers. The court and Mr. McGrath discuss the issue of immediate payment of the creditor's claim with the court advising that as Administrator, his client may choose to pay herself. The court advises Mr. McGrath that he should think about how to proceed as to the minor. Mr. McGrath reports of a possible petition to approve an accounting and will address the issue of the minor at that time and believes that the GAL should remain in place until then. Mr. Breen supports Mr. McGraths suggestion.

The Petition of Administrator, etc., is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Sarmiento. Not reported.

Petition Of Administrator Carmel Kavanagh To Approve Creditors Claim Of Administrator
Off Calendar Mar-16-2022 Continued To Apr-27-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John McGrath, in court, for Carmel Kavanagh, in court, Petitioner; Maureen McFadden, via zoom, for Tim Breen, via zoom, Guardian Ad Litem; James O'Brien, via zoom, for Aidan Kavanagh, Heir; Nancy Mass, via zoom, for Carmel Kavanagh in related family law matter.

The court confirms with Ms. McFadden and Mr. Obrien that they intend to object to this petition. The court advises counsel to formally file objections. Mr. O'Brien reports being unable to contact his client and would like an opportunity to do so prior to filing an objection or if unable to contact him to be relieved as counsel and requests a 30-day continuance. The court and counsel continue to discuss the history of this and the related family law matter with the court strongly urging the parties to attempt to resolve the matter via ADR.

After additional discussion, the Petition of Administrator, etc., is continued to Apr-27-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Counsel is instructed to formally file any written objections by Mar-30-2022 and any related responses are to be formally filed by Apr-13-2022.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

Off Calendar Oct-27-2021 Continued To Nov-24-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-26-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John McGrath, via ZOOM, counsel for Carmel Kavanagh, via ZOOM, Petitioner.

At the outset, Mr. McGrath advises the court that his client originally had a co-signer for an $18 million dollar bond orignally required by the court to grant the petition but that they have since declined to co-sign. Mr. McGrath then requests that the court grant the petition but to allow his client limited authority in order for to perform specific tasks including the following: 1) All tasks necessary to complete a pending sale of a single family home located in San Francisco at 86 San Jacinto Way; 2) Collect rent, pay expenses and perform necessary or needed maintenance at a multi-unit building located in San Francisco at 460 Natoma Street and 3) Collect rent, perform necessary or needed maintenance at a single condominium unit located in San Francisco at 163 Wood Street, #3.

After discussion, the Court takes this matter under submission with the intent to grant. Bond in the amount of 4,300,000.00 is required. Status on close of estate is set for October 25, 2022, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Tauscher. Not Reported. (D204)
